channel.title: duo — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by duo (duo).

item.title: CVE-2018-7340 · 7.7 HIGH — Multiple SAML libraries may allow authentication bypass via incorrect XML canonicalization and DOM traversal
item.description: Duo Network Gateway 1.2.9 and earlier may incorrectly utilize the results of XML DOM traversal and canonicalization APIs in such a way that an attacker may be able to manipulate the SAML data without invalidating the cryptographic signature, allowing…

item.title: CVE-2017-11430 · 7.7 HIGH — Multiple SAML libraries may allow authentication bypass via incorrect XML canonicalization and DOM traversal
item.description: OmniAuth OmnitAuth-SAML 1.9.0 and earlier may incorrectly utilize the results of XML DOM traversal and canonicalization APIs in such a way that an attacker may be able to manipulate the SAML data without invalidating the cryptographic signature, allo…

item.title: CVE-2017-11429 · 7.7 HIGH — Multiple SAML libraries may allow authentication bypass via incorrect XML canonicalization and DOM traversal
item.description: Clever saml2-js 2.0 and earlier may incorrectly utilize the results of XML DOM traversal and canonicalization APIs in such a way that an attacker may be able to manipulate the SAML data without invalidating the cryptographic signature, allowing the a…

item.title: CVE-2017-11428 · 7.7 HIGH — Multiple SAML libraries may allow authentication bypass via incorrect XML canonicalization and DOM traversal
item.description: OneLogin Ruby-SAML 1.6.0 and earlier may incorrectly utilize the results of XML DOM traversal and canonicalization APIs in such a way that an attacker may be able to manipulate the SAML data without invalidating the cryptographic signature, allowing…

item.title: CVE-2017-11427 · 7.7 HIGH — Multiple SAML libraries may allow authentication bypass via incorrect XML canonicalization and DOM traversal
item.description: OneLogin PythonSAML 2.3.0 and earlier may incorrectly utilize the results of XML DOM traversal and canonicalization APIs in such a way that an attacker may be able to manipulate the SAML data without invalidating the cryptographic signature, allowing…

item.title: CVE-2017-11424
item.description: In PyJWT 1.5.0 and below the `invalid_strings` check in `HMACAlgorithm.prepare_key` does not account for all PEM encoded public keys. Specifically, the PKCS1 PEM encoded format would be allowed because it is prefaced with the string `-----BEGIN RSA P…
